docs.title: PraisonAIBio
docs.text:
Open-source AI agents for systems biology — discover, simulate, and compare curated models from BioModels.org using PraisonAI.

For biologists and lab scientists. No heavy coding required.

docs.title: How it works
docs.text:
graph LR
    Q[Your question] --> A[AI agent]
    A --> B[(BioModels.org)]
    A --> R[Report or simulation]
  1. Ask a question in plain English.
  2. The agent searches BioModels.org.
  3. You get a shortlist, summary, or simulation preview.

docs.title: Try in 30 seconds
docs.text:
No AI (fastest) 
pip install -e "src/praisonai-bio"
python -c "import praisonai_bio"
python examples/small/01_search.py
With AI agent 
export OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...
python examples/big/01_find_models.py
YAML workflow 
praisonai workflow run workflows/cookbooks/glycolysis_demo.yaml

docs.title: What you can do
docs.text: Task Start here Install Install Copy-paste tasks Quick tasks Understand workflows For researchers See all tools Tools · Reference Run examples Examples

docs.title: Demo model
docs.text: BIOMD0000000206 — Teusink yeast glycolysis (used in cookbooks and benchmarks).

docs.title: Links
docs.text:
• GitHub
  • BioModels.org
  • PraisonAI

docs.title: praisonai-bio
docs.text:
pip install -e "src/praisonai-bio"
praisonai-bio init              # scaffold .praisonai/config.yaml + agents
praisonai-bio validate check    # package + toolsets OK
praisonai-bio validate repo     # full plan compliance (scripts/validate_repo.py)
praisonai-bio tools validate    # 21 entry points
praisonai-bio doctor mcp        # MCP + BASICO check

docs.title: PraisonAI wrapper
docs.text:
Correct patterns (not praisonai agent run):

praisonai run --agent biomodels-scout "Find glycolysis models"
praisonai workflow run workflows/cookbooks/glycolysis_demo.yaml
praisonai workflow run workflows/discovery/biomodels_discovery_pipeline.yaml
praisonai workflow run workflows/platform/policy_guarded_simulation.yaml

Requires pip install praisonai and python -c "import praisonai_bio".

docs.title: MCP servers
docs.text:
python mcp/sysbio-server/server.py       # 11 T2B tools
python mcp/biomodels-server/server.py    # read-only BioModels
praisonai-bio-mcp                        # console entry (if installed)

See MCP.

docs.title: Docs site
docs.text:
pip install -r docs/requirements.txt
mkdocs serve    # http://127.0.0.1:8000
mkdocs build    # output in site/

Live site: https://bio.praison.ai

docs.title: Custom domain — bio.praison.ai
docs.text: Docs are served at https://bio.praison.ai via GitHub Pages.

docs.title: 1. DNS (praison.ai registrar / Cloudflare)
docs.text:
Add a CNAME record:

Type Name Value TTL CNAME bio mervinpraison.github.io Auto

Note

Use bio as the host name (not bio.praison.ai in the name field on some panels).

If you use Cloudflare, proxy (orange cloud) is fine — enable Full SSL.

docs.title: 2. GitHub repo settings
docs.text:
1. Open PraisonAIBio → Settings → Pages
  2. Custom domain: bio.praison.ai
  3. Save — wait for DNS check (can take up to 24h, often minutes)
  4. Enable Enforce HTTPS when available

The deploy workflow writes CNAME on each push (cname: bio.praison.ai).

docs.title: 3. Repo files (already configured)
docs.text:
• docs/CNAME → copied into site/ on build
  • mkdocs.yml → site_url: https://bio.praison.ai/
  • .github/workflows/docs.yml → deploy with custom domain

docs.title: 4. Verify
docs.text:
dig bio.praison.ai CNAME +short
# expect: mervinpraison.github.io.

curl -I https://bio.praison.ai

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text: Issue Fix DNS not verified Wait; confirm CNAME points to mervinpraison.github.io Certificate pending Disable/re-enable Enforce HTTPS after DNS is green Wrong content Ensure Pages source is GitHub Actions, not a branch Old URL Redirect optional: keep site_url as bio.praison.ai only

docs.title: Development
docs.text: For contributors and CI.

docs.title: Setup
docs.text: pip install -e "src/praisonai-bio[dev,simulation]"

docs.title: Tests
docs.text:
./scripts/test_all.sh

Or step by step:

pytest tests/unit -q
pytest tests/integration -q      # live BioModels.org
pytest tests/agentic -q          # needs OPENAI_API_KEY

docs.title: Validation
docs.text:
python scripts/validate_repo.py
bash scripts/check_no_submission.sh
python scripts/generate_catalog.py

docs.title: Docs site
docs.text:
pip install -r docs/requirements.txt
mkdocs serve    # http://127.0.0.1:8000
mkdocs build    # output in site/

docs.title: Package layout
docs.text:
src/praisonai-bio/praisonai_bio/
  adapters/     # BioModels API, BASICO
  tools/        # @tool functions + entry points
  toolsets/     # prebuilt toolsets
  workflows/    # (YAML lives in repo workflows/)

See AGENTS.md on GitHub for contributor rules.

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
See the full examples catalogue with run commands and sample output:

→ Examples catalogue

  • Small — no AI
  • Big — AI agent

Regenerate captured outputs:

./scripts/capture_example_outputs.sh

docs.title: Do I need to code?
docs.text: No for small examples and YAML workflows. Agent examples need an API key but not writing code.

docs.title: What is BIOMD0000000206?
docs.text: The demo model — Teusink yeast glycolysis. Used in cookbooks and docs.

docs.title: Why import praisonai_bio?
docs.text: Toolsets register at import time. YAML/CLI will not find sysbio-full without it.

docs.title: Simulation fails / BASICO missing
docs.text:
pip install "praisonai-bio[simulation]"

Discovery and search work without BASICO.

docs.title: BioModels vs EBI URLs?
docs.text: PraisonAIBio uses https://www.biomodels.org by default. Override with BIOMODELS_BASE_URL.

docs.title: How is this different from Talk2BioModels?
docs.text:
T2B focuses on simulation after you pick a model. PraisonAIBio adds discovery, ranking, workflows, and multi-agent orchestration.

Compare →

docs.title: How is this different from ClawBio?
docs.text:
ClawBio is genomics skills. PraisonAIBio is BioModels / SBML simulation.

Compare →

docs.title: For researchers
docs.text: PraisonAIBio helps you find, check, and simulate models from BioModels.org — without browsing thousands of entries by hand.

docs.title: The problem it solves
docs.text: Manual work With PraisonAIBio Weeks browsing BioModels Minutes: search + ranked shortlist Reading papers for assumptions Agent summary + trust score Setting up COPASI by hand Optional one-command simulation

docs.title: A — Direct tools (small examples)
docs.text:
You run a short script. No chat.

→ Best for: quick lookup, one model ID, validation.

→ Folder: examples/small/

docs.title: B — AI agent (big examples)
docs.text:
You ask in English. The agent picks tools.

→ Best for: open questions, comparing models, reports.

→ Folder: examples/big/

docs.title: Typical study flow
docs.text:
graph TD
    A[Research question] --> B[Search BioModels]
    B --> C[Rank & trust score]
    C --> D[Assumption review]
    D --> E{Happy?}
    E -->|Yes| F[Simulate]
    E -->|No| B
    F --> G[Compare & report]

YAML workflows in workflows/discovery/ automate this team.

Workflow file What it does biomodels_discovery_pipeline.yaml Question → shortlist biomodels_assumption_review.yaml Check assumptions (human approval step) biomodels_baseline_simulation.yaml Run baseline simulation biomodels_full_research_workflow.yaml All phases together

Run any workflow:

praisonai workflow run workflows/discovery/biomodels_discovery_pipeline.yaml

docs.title: Demo model to try
docs.text:
BIOMD0000000206 — yeast glycolysis (Teusink et al.)

Used in cookbooks, examples, and benchmarks.

docs.title: Need help choosing?
docs.text:
If you… Use Know the model ID examples/small/02_model_info.py Have a pathway name only examples/big/01_find_models.py Want a full pipeline workflows/cookbooks/glycolysis_demo.yaml

See Tools at a glance and Tools reference for the full tool list and parameters.

docs.title: Get started
docs.text: Three steps. No biology coding required.

docs.title: Step 1 — Install
docs.text:
cd PraisonAIBio
pip install -e "src/praisonai-bio"
python -c "import praisonai_bio"

docs.title: Step 2 — Try a search (no AI)
docs.text:
python examples/small/01_search.py

You get: a list of curated models matching “glycolysis”.

docs.title: Step 3 — Ask an agent (optional)
docs.text:
Set your OpenAI key, then:

export OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...
python examples/big/01_find_models.py

You get: a short answer with recommended model IDs.

docs.title: What next?
docs.text:
Goal Command Check one model python examples/small/02_model_info.py Trust / curation score python examples/small/03_trust_score.py Validate SBML python examples/small/04_validate_sbml.py Full glycolysis walkthrough python examples/big/04_glycolysis_demo.py YAML workflow (no Python) praisonai workflow run workflows/cookbooks/glycolysis_demo.yaml

Open Quick tasks for a task menu.

docs.title: From GitHub (development)
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/MervinPraison/PraisonAIBio.git
cd PraisonAIBio
pip install -e "src/praisonai-bio"
python -c "import praisonai_bio"

docs.title: From PyPI (when published)
docs.text:
pip install praisonai-bio
python -c "import praisonai_bio"

Note

PyPI releases track tagged versions on GitHub. Until the first publish, use the GitHub install above.

Required once per session

Always run import praisonai_bio before YAML or CLI toolsets.

docs.title: Optional extras
docs.text:
Simulation (BASICO/COPASI) 
pip install -e "src/praisonai-bio[simulation]"
# or: pip install "praisonai-bio[simulation]"
Plotting 
pip install -e "src/praisonai-bio[plot]"
Everything 
pip install -e "src/praisonai-bio[all,dev]"

docs.title: PraisonAI CLI (workflows)
docs.text:
Install the PraisonAI wrapper for YAML workflows and agents:

pip install praisonai

Scaffold local agent config:

praisonai-bio init   # creates .praisonai/config.yaml + agent templates

docs.title: Verify
docs.text:
praisonai-bio validate check
praisonai-bio tools validate

Expected: 21 tools, toolsets including sysbio-full.

docs.title: Environment
docs.text: Variable Purpose OPENAI_API_KEY Agent examples (big/) BIOMODELS_BASE_URL Default https://www.biomodels.org TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN Optional — botos_sysbio_telegram.yaml workflow

docs.title: Documentation site
docs.text:
Live docs: https://bio.praison.ai

Local preview:

pip install -r docs/requirements.txt
mkdocs serve

Next: Get started

docs.title: Plan validation report
docs.text:
Multi-agent audit of the PraisonAIBio master plan against the repository (Phase 0/1).

Run automated checks:

python scripts/validate_repo.py
./scripts/test_all.sh

docs.title: Summary
docs.text: Area Status Notes Package + tools PASS 21 tools, 10 toolsets, entry points OK Workflows (scaffold) PASS Discovery, orchestration, platform, cookbooks Skills + MCP PASS 5 skills; MCP exposes 11 T2B tools Docs + examples PASS MkDocs, captured outputs, skills guide Session persistence PASS repro_export writes to ~/.praisonai/biomodels-runs/ Discovery route PASS route: after domain classifier HITL in full pipeline PARTIAL Gate via linked platform workflow (see below) Handoffs PASS Agent handoffs: in full research workflow Recipes PASS TEMPLATE.yaml per recipe Benchmark ground truth PARTIAL Generate with scripts/generate_ground_truth.py MCP resources/prompts DEFERRED Markdown spec only; Phase 2 server wiring Knowledge/RAG STUB article_index.py returns indexed: False Phase 2 items DEFERRED OLS, Docker, ClawBio bridge, PyPI

docs.title: CONTRIBUTING + ROADMAP
docs.text: Added at repo root per plan §802–805.

docs.title: Session persistence
docs.text:
repro_export(model_id, output_dir, run_id=...) saves manifest JSON under:

~/.praisonai/biomodels-runs/{run_id}/repro_manifest.json

docs.title: HITL between assumption review and simulation
docs.text:
The full 7-phase workflow references the platform gate:

# Phase 3 → HITL → Phase 4
praisonai workflow run workflows/platform/approval_assumption_gate.yaml
praisonai workflow run workflows/discovery/biomodels_full_research_workflow.yaml --var question="..."

The full research YAML includes an inline approve step where the workflow engine supports it; the platform gate remains the canonical HITL path for non-interactive CI.

docs.title: Route in discovery pipeline
docs.text: workflows/discovery/biomodels_discovery_pipeline.yaml includes a route: step after domain classification (pathway / disease / drug / general).

docs.title: Handoffs
docs.text: workflows/discovery/biomodels_full_research_workflow.yaml agents declare handoffs: for intake → scout → analyst → simulation chain.

docs.title: Recipes
docs.text:
Each recipe under recipes/bio/*/TEMPLATE.yaml points to the workflow file for praisonai recipe run:

praisonai recipe run /path/to/PraisonAIBio/recipes/bio/biomodels-discovery

docs.title: Ground truth trajectories
docs.text:
pip install "praisonai-bio[simulation]"
python scripts/generate_ground_truth.py
# writes benchmarks/t2b_parity/ground_truth/BIOMD0000000206_trajectory.csv

docs.title: Phase 2 (intentionally deferred)
docs.text:
• ols_adapter.py
  • mcp/sysbio-server/Dockerfile
  • Full ClawBio bridge
  • BotOS production deployment
  • PyPI publish

docs.title: Last validated
docs.text: Re-run python scripts/validate_repo.py after any structural change.

docs.title: Quick tasks
docs.text: Pick a task. Copy the command. Run it.

docs.title: Task 1 — Find models
docs.text:
Question: “What glycolysis models exist?”

python examples/small/01_search.py

No API key needed.

docs.title: Task 2 — Inspect one model
docs.text:
Question: “What is BIOMD0000000206?”

python examples/small/02_model_info.py

Demo model: Teusink yeast glycolysis.

docs.title: Task 3 — Is this model trustworthy?
docs.text:
Question: “Is this model curated and published?”

python examples/small/03_trust_score.py

docs.title: Task 3b — Validate SBML structure
docs.text:
Question: “Is BIOMD0000000206 structurally valid?”

python examples/small/04_validate_sbml.py

docs.title: Task 4 — Let AI search for you
docs.text:
Question: “Find MAPK models for me.”

export OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...
python examples/big/01_find_models.py

docs.title: Task 5 — Full discovery study
docs.text:
Question: “Which model should I use for MAPK signalling?”

export OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...
python examples/big/03_discovery_study.py

docs.title: Task 6 — Glycolysis demo (agent)
docs.text:
export OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...
python examples/big/04_glycolysis_demo.py

docs.title: Task 6b — Agent summarises one model
docs.text:
export OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...
python examples/big/02_summarise_model.py

docs.title: Task 7 — Workflow file (multi-step team)
docs.text:
No Python — uses PraisonAI workflow YAML:

praisonai workflow run workflows/cookbooks/glycolysis_demo.yaml

Agents work in sequence: find → summarise → simulate.

docs.title: Task 8 — Reproducibility bundle
docs.text:
python -c "
import praisonai_bio
from praisonai_bio.tools.repro_export import repro_export
print(repro_export.run(model_id='BIOMD0000000206', output_dir='/tmp/glycolysis_bundle'))
"

Creates model.sbml, metadata, and commands.sh in the folder.

docs.title: Task 9 — Policy-guarded simulation (workflow)
docs.text:
export OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...
python -c "import praisonai_bio"
praisonai workflow run workflows/platform/policy_guarded_simulation.yaml

See Workflows — Platform.

docs.title: Recipes
docs.text:
Packaged workflows under recipes/bio/ — same automation as YAML workflows, with metadata for discovery.

Recipe Folder Workflow biomodels-discovery recipes/bio/biomodels-discovery/ Discovery pipeline → shortlist biomodels-research recipes/bio/biomodels-research/ Full research workflow biomodels-simulate recipes/bio/biomodels-simulate/ Baseline simulation biomodels-perturb recipes/bio/biomodels-perturb/ Perturbation study biomodels-report recipes/bio/biomodels-report/ Comparison report

Each folder contains recipe.yaml pointing at a workflow under workflows/discovery/.

docs.title: Run (recommended)
docs.text:
Recipes wrap existing workflows — run the workflow directly:

cd PraisonAIBio
python -c "import praisonai_bio"

# Discovery shortlist
praisonai workflow run workflows/discovery/biomodels_discovery_pipeline.yaml

# Full research (7 phases)
praisonai workflow run workflows/discovery/biomodels_full_research_workflow.yaml

# Baseline simulation
praisonai workflow run workflows/discovery/biomodels_baseline_simulation.yaml

# Perturbation study
praisonai workflow run workflows/discovery/biomodels_perturbation_study.yaml

# Comparison report
praisonai workflow run workflows/discovery/biomodels_comparison_report.yaml

docs.title: Recipe path (optional)
docs.text:
Point PraisonAI at the recipe catalog:

export PRAISONAI_RECIPE_PATH="$(pwd)/recipes/bio"

Then use PraisonAI recipe commands if your wrapper version supports them; otherwise use the workflow paths above.

docs.title: Cookbooks
docs.text:
Ready-made demos in workflows/cookbooks/:

praisonai workflow run workflows/cookbooks/glycolysis_demo.yaml
praisonai workflow run workflows/cookbooks/mapk_p53_discovery.yaml

Demo model: BIOMD0000000206 (yeast glycolysis).

See Workflows and For researchers.

docs.title: Skills
docs.text:
Cursor / agent instruction packs for BioModels tasks — ClawBio-style layout, lighter than full ClawBio skills (no separate Python runner required; uses PraisonAI toolsets).

Catalog: skills/catalog.json

docs.title: Available skills
docs.text:
Skill ID Use when Search BioModels search-models Find curated models for a pathway or keyword Load BioModels SBML load-biomodel Download and inspect SBML on disk Simulate Model simulate-model Run BASICO time-course or steady state SysBio Orchestrator sysbio-orchestrator Multi-step discovery → simulate pipeline Reproducibility Enforcer repro-enforcer Export repro bundles with checksums

Each skill lives in skills/<id>/SKILL.md.

docs.title: PraisonAIBio vs ClawBio skills
docs.text:
PraisonAIBio ClawBio (~/ClawBio) Purpose Systems biology / BioModels / SBML Genomics, WES, RNA-seq, etc. Implementation Markdown + PraisonAI @tool functions Often SKILL.md + Python script + tests Registration skills/catalog.json via generate_catalog.py catalog.json + clawbio.py aliases Scaffold Copy SKILL-TEMPLATE.md Use skill-builder meta-skill Bridge mcp/clawbio-bridge/ (v2) N/A

For heavy genomics pipelines, use ClawBio directly. For BioModels discovery and simulation, use PraisonAIBio skills + toolsets.

docs.title: How to add a new skill
1. Create the folder
docs.text:
Use a kebab-case id (matches directory name):

mkdir -p skills/my-skill-id
cp skills/SKILL-TEMPLATE.md skills/my-skill-id/SKILL.md

docs.title: 2. Edit SKILL.md
docs.text:
Required sections (see template):

  1. Title — first line # Title (used as display name in catalog)
  2. When to use — trigger scenarios for the agent
  3. Tools — list tools with parameters (agents only see parameters, not env vars)
  4. Toolset — which prebuilt toolset to attach (biomodels-readonly, simulation, …)
  5. Example — minimal Agent snippet loading this skill as instructions

Optional: prerequisites ([simulation] extra), demo model ID.

Tip

Copy an existing skill as a starting point: cp -r skills/search-models skills/my-skill-id

docs.title: 3. Regenerate the catalog
docs.text:
python scripts/generate_catalog.py

This scans skills/*/SKILL.md and rewrites skills/catalog.json.

docs.title: 4. Validate
docs.text:
python scripts/validate_repo.py   # skills section must stay OK
python -c "import praisonai_bio"
praisonai-bio validate check

docs.title: 5. Test with an agent
docs.text:
import praisonai_bio
from praisonaiagents import Agent

agent = Agent(
    name="test",
    instructions=open("skills/my-skill-id/SKILL.md").read(),
    toolsets=["biomodels-readonly"],  # match your SKILL.md
    llm="gpt-4o-mini",
)
print(agent.start("Your test prompt"))

docs.title: 6. Document and submit
docs.text:
• Add a row to the table above (this page) if the skill is public
  • Open a pull request with SKILL.md, updated catalog.json, and any tests

Optional extras (like ClawBio, but not required for PraisonAIBio):

skills/my-skill-id/
├── SKILL.md           # required
├── examples/          # optional sample prompts or output
└── tests/             # optional pytest for helper scripts

docs.title: Use with Cursor
docs.text:
1. Copy or symlink the skill folder into your Cursor skills directory, or
  2. Reference the repo path in chat:
Read skills/search-models/SKILL.md and search BioModels for MAPK models.

docs.title: Use with PraisonAI agents
docs.text:
Skills supply instructions; toolsets supply capabilities:

import praisonai_bio
from praisonaiagents import Agent

agent = Agent(
    name="scout",
    instructions=open("skills/search-models/SKILL.md").read(),
    toolsets=["biomodels-readonly"],
    llm="gpt-4o-mini",
)
agent.start("Find curated p53 models")

Always run import praisonai_bio first.

docs.title: YAML workflows
docs.text:
Point agent instructions at a skill file in .praisonai/agents/ or inline in workflow YAML:

agents:
  scout:
    role: BioModels Scout
    instructions_file: skills/search-models/SKILL.md
    toolsets:
      - biomodels-readonly

(If your PraisonAI version uses instructions: instead of instructions_file:, paste or load the markdown content.)

docs.title: See also
docs.text:
• Toolsets
  • Tools reference
  • Workflows
  • ClawBio skill builder: ~/ClawBio/skills/skill-builder/SKILL.md

docs.title: Tools at a glance
docs.text: What each tool does — no code required to understand.

docs.title: Discovery (safe, read-only)
docs.text: Tool You ask… You get… search_models “Find p53 models” List from BioModels.org get_modelinfo “Details for BIOMD…” Name, authors, description rank_models “Best fit for my question?” Ranked list with scores trust_scorecard “Is this curated?” Trust score + reasons compare_models “How do A and B differ?” Side-by-side metadata sbml_summarise “What’s in the model?” Species, reactions summary sbml_validate “Is the SBML valid?” Structure and unit check sedml_parse “What SED-ML tasks exist?” Tasks and data generators get_annotation “What databases link to this?” MIRIAM annotations query_article “Which paper?” DOI / PubMed links

docs.title: Simulation (needs pip install praisonai-bio[simulation])
docs.text: Tool You ask… You get… load_biomodel “Load this ID” SBML on disk simulate_model “Run for 100 time units” Time-course preview steady_state “Steady state?” Concentrations parameter_scan “Scan parameter X” Scan table preview_outcomes “Quick preview” Short simulation simulate_perturbation “Change one parameter” Perturbed run custom_plotter “Plot results” PNG file

docs.title: Reporting & reproducibility
docs.text: Tool You ask… You get… compare_simulations “Baseline vs perturbed?” Comparison summary save_model “Save SBML” File on disk repro_export “Repro bundle” Folder with SBML + checksums ask_question “Explain this model” Metadata for the agent to reason over

docs.title: Toolsets (bundles)
docs.text:
Toolset Who it's for biomodels-readonly Discovery only — safe sbml_analysis Load, summarise, validate SBML sysbio-core Discovery + basic simulation simulation Running models (BASICO) reporting Compare models and simulations repro Reproducibility bundles sysbio-full All 21 tools sysbio-orchestrator Multi-step discovery agent sysbio-safe Read-only, no writes or simulation genomics-bridge v2 stub — register ClawBio tools at runtime

Use in agents: toolsets=["biomodels-readonly"]

Full parameter list: Tools reference

Always run import praisonai_bio first.

docs.title: Tools reference
docs.text:
All 21 tools with parameters agents can use. Provide every option explicitly in prompts or YAML — agents do not read env vars or config files.

Tip

Run python -c "import praisonai_bio" before calling tools from agents or workflows.

docs.title: Discovery (read-only)
docs.text: Tool Parameters Returns search_models query (str), num_results (int, default 10), offset (int, default 0), curated_only (bool, default true) JSON list of matching models get_modelinfo model_id (str, e.g. BIOMD0000000206) Name, authors, description rank_models query (str), num_results (int, default 10), research_question (str, optional) Ranked models with scores trust_scorecard model_id (str) Curation score and reasons compare_models model_id_a (str), model_id_b (str) Side-by-side metadata get_annotation model_id (str) MIRIAM / RDF annotations query_article model_id (str) DOI, PubMed, citation links ask_question model_id (str), question (str) Metadata-grounded answer text sbml_summarise model_id (str) Species, compartments, reactions summary sbml_validate model_id (str, optional), sbml_path (str, optional) — one required Validation report (structure, units) sedml_parse model_id (str), filename (str, optional SED-ML file in bundle) SED-ML tasks and data generators

docs.title: Simulation (needs pip install praisonai-bio[simulation])
docs.text: Tool Parameters Returns load_biomodel model_id (str), save_path (str, optional) Path to downloaded SBML simulate_model model_id (str, optional), sbml_path (str, optional), duration (float, default 100), step (float, default 0.1) Time-course JSON preview steady_state model_id (str, optional), sbml_path (str, optional) Steady-state concentrations parameter_scan model_id (str), parameter (str), start (float), end (float), steps (int, default 10) Scan table JSON preview_outcomes model_id (str), duration (float, default 50) Short baseline simulation simulate_perturbation model_id (str), parameter (str), value (float), duration (float, default 100) Perturbed time-course JSON custom_plotter data_json (str), x_column (str), y_column (str), title (str, default "Simulation plot"), output_path (str, default plot.png) PNG path (needs [plot])

docs.title: Reporting & reproducibility
docs.text: Tool Parameters Returns compare_simulations baseline_json (str), perturbation_json (str) Comparison summary JSON save_model model_id (str), output_path (str) Saved SBML path repro_export model_id (str), output_dir (str), run_id (str, optional) Bundle dir with SBML, metadata, commands.sh, checksums

docs.title: Python example
docs.text:
import praisonai_bio
from praisonai_bio.tools.search_models import search_models
from praisonai_bio.tools.sbml_validate import sbml_validate

print(search_models.run(query="glycolysis", num_results=5))
print(sbml_validate.run(model_id="BIOMD0000000206"))

docs.title: See also
docs.text:
• Tools at a glance — plain-language summary
  • Toolsets — named bundles for agents
  • Quick tasks — copy-paste commands

docs.title: Toolsets
docs.text:
Toolsets are named bundles of tools for agents and YAML workflows.

Important

Run python -c "import praisonai_bio" before using toolsets in YAML or CLI.

docs.title: Toolsets
docs.text: Toolset Use when biomodels-readonly Search and metadata only (safe) sbml_analysis Load and summarise SBML sysbio-core Discovery + basic simulation simulation Run models (needs BASICO) reporting Compare and report sysbio-full All 21 tools sysbio-orchestrator Multi-step discovery agent sysbio-safe Read-only, no writes or simulation repro Reproducibility bundles genomics-bridge v2 stub — ClawBio genomics bridge (empty until external tools registered)

docs.title: Python
docs.text:
import praisonai_bio
from praisonaiagents import Agent

agent = Agent(
    name="scout",
    toolsets=["biomodels-readonly"],
    llm="gpt-4o-mini",
)
agent.start("Find curated MAPK models")

docs.title: YAML
docs.text:
agents:
  scout:
    role: BioModels Scout
    toolsets:
      - biomodels-readonly

docs.title: Tools in sysbio-full
docs.text:
T2B (11): search_models, get_modelinfo, load_biomodel, simulate_model, ask_question, steady_state, parameter_scan, custom_plotter, get_annotation, query_article, save_model

Discovery: rank_models, trust_scorecard, compare_models, preview_outcomes, sbml_summarise, sbml_validate, sedml_parse, simulate_perturbation, compare_simulations, repro_export

Details: Tools at a glance · Tools reference

docs.title: MCP servers
docs.text: Expose PraisonAIBio tools to Cursor, Claude Desktop, and other MCP clients.

docs.title: Servers
docs.text:
Server Path Scope sysbio mcp/sysbio-server/server.py 11 T2B simulation tools biomodels mcp/biomodels-server/server.py Read-only search and metadata Package praisonai-bio-mcp Same as sysbio (console entry point) ClawBio bridge mcp/clawbio-bridge/bridge.py v2 — connect genomics context to pathway tools

Discovery tools

MCP servers expose the 11 Talk2BioModels tools. The extra 10 discovery tools (rank_models, sbml_validate, repro_export, etc.) are available via Python toolsets and YAML workflows, not MCP yet.

docs.title: Run locally
docs.text:
pip install -e "src/praisonai-bio[simulation]"
python -c "import praisonai_bio"
python mcp/sysbio-server/server.py

Read-only (no BASICO):

python mcp/biomodels-server/server.py

Health check:

praisonai-bio doctor mcp

docs.title: PraisonAI integration
docs.text:
praisonai mcp add   # register server in config
praisonai serve mcp

Workflow stub: workflows/platform/mcp_sysbio_server.yaml

docs.title: Resources and prompts
docs.text: File Purpose mcp/sysbio-server/resources.md biomodel://{id}, demo://glycolysis URIs mcp/sysbio-server/prompts.md Pre-built MCP prompts

docs.title: Cursor config example
docs.text:
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "praisonai-sysbio": {
      "command": "python",
      "args": ["/path/to/PraisonAIBio/mcp/sysbio-server/server.py"]
    }
  }
}

Run from repo root after pip install -e "src/praisonai-bio[simulation]".

See Tools reference for all 21 Python tools.

docs.title: PraisonAIBio vs ClawBio
docs.text:
Capability ClawBio PraisonAIBio Domain Genomics / clinical bioinformatics Systems biology / SBML BioModels.org No Core ODE simulation No BASICO/COPASI Multi-agent workflows External LLM Native PraisonAI

PraisonAIBio complements ClawBio — v2 mcp/clawbio-bridge/ connects genomics context to pathway simulation.

docs.title: PraisonAIBio vs Talk2BioModels
docs.text:
Talk2BioModels (T2B) provides 11 simulation-focused tools after a model is chosen.

PraisonAIBio adds:

  • Automated discovery and ranking on BioModels.org
  • Multi-agent YAML workflows (Route, Parallel, Loop, HITL)
  • Trust scorecards and assumption review
  • CLI / YAML / Python parity via PraisonAI

All 11 T2B tools are replicated as PraisonAI @tool functions with entry-point registration.

docs.title: Workflows
docs.text: Multi-agent YAML workflows — no Python required.

docs.title: Discovery (workflows/discovery/)
docs.text:
File What it does biomodels_discovery_pipeline.yaml Question → ranked shortlist biomodels_assumption_review.yaml Assumption review + human approval biomodels_baseline_simulation.yaml Baseline simulation biomodels_perturbation_study.yaml Perturbation scenarios biomodels_comparison_report.yaml Comparison report biomodels_full_research_workflow.yaml All phases (7 steps) 
praisonai workflow run workflows/discovery/biomodels_discovery_pipeline.yaml

docs.title: Cookbooks (workflows/cookbooks/)
docs.text: File Demo glycolysis_demo.yaml BIOMD0000000206 end-to-end mapk_p53_discovery.yaml MAPK / p53 shortlist

docs.title: Orchestration patterns
docs.text: File Pattern sysbio_route_by_input.yaml Route by domain parallel_sensitivity_scan.yaml Parallel scans team_model_review.yaml AgentTeam review full_platform_showcase.yaml Route + Parallel + Memory precision_medicine_loop.yaml Loop: baseline → perturb → compare

docs.title: Platform (workflows/platform/)
docs.text:
Production patterns — policy, approval, eval, MCP, bots.

File What it does approval_assumption_gate.yaml Human approval before assumption review approval_parameter_scan.yaml Human approval before parameter scan policy_guarded_simulation.yaml Simulation with export path policy eval_regression_gate.yaml Eval gate on tool selection mcp_sysbio_server.yaml Run sysbio MCP server workflow botos_sysbio_telegram.yaml Telegram bot + sysbio tools (needs token) botos_stub.yaml BotOS wiring stub 
praisonai workflow run workflows/platform/policy_guarded_simulation.yaml

Agent defaults: workflows/agents/defaults.yaml

docs.title: Flow (full research)
docs.text:
graph TD
    A[Intake] --> B[Search BioModels]
    B --> C[Assumption review]
    C --> D[Simulate]
    D --> E[Report]

Before running: python -c "import praisonai_bio"

See For researchers and Recipes.

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: Runnable scripts with recorded sample output (captured from live runs against BioModels.org).

docs.title: Before you run
docs.text:
cd PraisonAIBio
pip install -e "src/praisonai-bio"
python -c "import praisonai_bio"

Agent examples (big/) also need:

export OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...
pip install -e "src/praisonai-bio[simulation]"   # optional — for simulation in glycolysis demo

Regenerate all saved outputs:

./scripts/capture_example_outputs.sh

docs.title: Small — direct tools (no AI)
docs.text:
No API key. One BioModels call per script.

Example Script Docs Search models examples/small/01_search.py 01 — Search Model metadata examples/small/02_model_info.py 02 — Model info Trust score examples/small/03_trust_score.py 03 — Trust score Validate SBML examples/small/04_validate_sbml.py 04 — Validate SBML

docs.title: Big — AI agent
docs.text:
Uses gpt-4o-mini and PraisonAI toolsets.

Example Script Docs Find models examples/big/01_find_models.py 01 — Find models Summarise model examples/big/02_summarise_model.py 02 — Summarise Discovery study examples/big/03_discovery_study.py 03 — Discovery Glycolysis demo examples/big/04_glycolysis_demo.py 04 — Glycolysis

Agent output varies

LLM replies differ slightly each run. Saved output.txt files show a real capture; your text may differ but structure should match.

docs.title: YAML workflows
docs.text:
praisonai workflow run workflows/cookbooks/glycolysis_demo.yaml

See Workflows.

docs.title: Example — Agent finds models
docs.text:
Category: Big (AI agent) · Script: examples/big/01_find_models.py

An agent searches BioModels.org using the biomodels-readonly toolset.

docs.title: How to run
docs.text:
pip install -e "src/praisonai-bio"
export OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...
python -c "import praisonai_bio"
python examples/big/01_find_models.py

docs.title: Sample output
docs.text:
Here are the curated yeast glycolysis model IDs:

1. BIOMD0000000471
2. BIOMD0000000472
3. BIOMD0000000473
4. BIOMD0000000691
5. BIOMD0000000064
6. BIOMD0000000254
7. BIOMD0000000172

The agent calls search_models internally, then formats IDs for you.

Full captured output

output.txt

← All examples

docs.title: Example — Agent summarises a model
docs.text:
Category: Big (AI agent) · Script: examples/big/02_summarise_model.py

Plain-language summary of BIOMD0000000206 for biologists.

docs.title: How to run
docs.text:
pip install -e "src/praisonai-bio"
export OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...
python -c "import praisonai_bio"
python examples/big/02_summarise_model.py

Uses toolset sbml_analysis (get_modelinfo, sbml_summarise, etc.).

docs.title: Sample output
docs.text:
The model Wolf2000_Glycolytic_Oscillations simulates the dynamics of ATP and NADH
in yeast glycolysis, particularly showcasing the oscillatory behavior of these
molecules. It successfully reproduces the patterns seen in a figure from a
supporting paper and was tested using software tools Jarnac and MathSBML.

This model uses ordinary differential equations to describe the interactions
within the glycolytic process. It highlights how populations of yeast cells can
exhibit synchronized glycolytic oscillations due to intercellular signaling
through acetaldehyde.

Key publication details include:
- Title: Transduction of intracellular and intercellular dynamics in yeast
  glycolytic oscillations.
- Authors: Jana Wolf et al.
- Published in: Biophysical Journal, March 2000.
Full captured output

output.txt

← All examples

docs.title: Example — Discovery study (MAPK)
docs.text:
Category: Big (AI agent) · Script: examples/big/03_discovery_study.py

Search, rank, and recommend the best curated model for a research question.

docs.title: How to run
docs.text:
pip install -e "src/praisonai-bio"
export OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...
python -c "import praisonai_bio"
python examples/big/03_discovery_study.py

Uses toolset sysbio-orchestrator (search_models, rank_models, trust_scorecard, …).

docs.title: Sample output
docs.text:
The best curated MAPK model for cell signaling studies is:

### Sarma2012 - Oscillations in MAPK cascade (S2), inclusion of external signaling module
- ID: BIOMD0000000442
- Submitter: Uddipan Sarma
- Submission Date: 2011-12-19
- Relevance Score: 3

This model provides a comprehensive framework for studying MAPK behavior
in a signaling context.
Full captured output

output.txt

← All examples

docs.title: Example — Glycolysis walkthrough
docs.text:
Category: Big (AI agent) · Script: examples/big/04_glycolysis_demo.py

End-to-end walkthrough of BIOMD0000000206 — find, summarise, and preview simulation.

docs.title: How to run
docs.text:
pip install -e "src/praisonai-bio[simulation]"
export OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...
python -c "import praisonai_bio"
python examples/big/04_glycolysis_demo.py

Uses toolset sysbio-core. Simulation preview needs BASICO ([simulation] extra).

docs.title: Sample output
docs.text:
The Teusink Yeast Glycolysis Model investigates glycolytic oscillations in
Saccharomyces cerevisiae.

Model Overview
- Title: Wolf2000 Glycolytic Oscillations
- Purpose: oscillatory dynamics of ATP and NADH; synchronization via acetaldehyde

Simulation Outcomes (100 time units)
- ATP: fluctuates ~1.6 to 3.0 (periodic oscillations)
- NAD: oscillatory behaviour from ~0.6
- Acetaldehyde: minor fluctuations as signaling metabolite

With [simulation] installed, the agent can call preview_outcomes or simulate_model and report time-course values.

Full captured output

output.txt

docs.title: Related workflow
docs.text:
Same demo as YAML (3 agent steps):

praisonai workflow run workflows/cookbooks/glycolysis_demo.yaml

← All examples

docs.title: Example — Search BioModels
docs.text:
Category: Small (no AI) · Script: examples/small/01_search.py

Search curated glycolysis models on BioModels.org.

docs.title: How to run
docs.text:
pip install -e "src/praisonai-bio"
python -c "import praisonai_bio"
python examples/small/01_search.py

No API key required. Needs network access to BioModels.org.

docs.title: Sample output
docs.text:
Returns JSON with a curated search query and matching models:

{
  "query": "glycolysis AND curationstatus:\"Manually curated\"",
  "count": 10,
  "models": [
    {
      "id": "BIOMD0000000471",
      "name": "Smallbone2013 - Yeast metabolic model with linlog rate law",
      "url": "https://www.biomodels.org/BIOMD0000000471"
    },
    {
      "id": "BIOMD0000000472",
      "name": "Smallbone2013 - Yeast metabolic model with modular rate law",
      "url": "https://www.biomodels.org/BIOMD0000000472"
    }
  ]
}
Full captured output

Full JSON is saved in the repo: output.txt

cat docs/examples/small/01-search/output.txt

← All examples

docs.title: Example — Model metadata
docs.text:
Category: Small (no AI) · Script: examples/small/02_model_info.py

Fetch full metadata for demo model BIOMD0000000206 (Teusink yeast glycolysis).

docs.title: How to run
docs.text:
pip install -e "src/praisonai-bio"
python -c "import praisonai_bio"
python examples/small/02_model_info.py

docs.title: Sample output
docs.text:
JSON metadata including name, submitter, publication links, and MIRIAM annotations:

{
  "id": "BIOMD0000000206",
  "name": "Wolf2000_Glycolytic_Oscillations",
  "submitter": "Nicolas Le Novère",
  "description": "Model reproducing glycolytic oscillations in yeast..."
}

The live response includes publication, authors, curationStatus, and qualifiers arrays.

Full captured output

output.txt (~12 KB JSON)

python examples/small/02_model_info.py | head -40

docs.title: Example — Trust scorecard
docs.text:
Category: Small (no AI) · Script: examples/small/03_trust_score.py

Score how trustworthy a BioModels entry is (curation, publication, metadata completeness).

docs.title: How to run
docs.text:
pip install -e "src/praisonai-bio"
python -c "import praisonai_bio"
python examples/small/03_trust_score.py

Uses model BIOMD0000000206.

docs.title: Sample output
docs.text:
{
  "model_id": "BIOMD0000000206",
  "score": 85,
  "grade": "high",
  "reasons": [
    "Manually curated on BioModels.org",
    "Linked publication metadata present",
    "MIRIAM annotations available"
  ],
  "curation_status": "Manually curated"
}

Exact score fields depend on live BioModels metadata.

docs.title: Example — Validate SBML
docs.text:
Category: Small (no AI) · Script: examples/small/04_validate_sbml.py

Download SBML from BioModels and check structure (species, reactions, basic validity). No simulation required.

docs.title: How to run
docs.text:
pip install -e "src/praisonai-bio"
python -c "import praisonai_bio"
python examples/small/04_validate_sbml.py

docs.title: Sample output
docs.text:
{
  "valid": true,
  "species_count": 9,
  "reaction_count": 11,
  "issues": []
}

If validation fails, valid is false and issues lists problems found.
